title: Automated Cyber Risk Quantification: FAIR Model Implementation with Splunk ES and MITRE Caldera Simulations
author: Sandhya Guduru
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper presents an integrated approach to automated cyber risk quantification by combining the Factor Analysis of Information Risk (FAIR) model with Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) and MITRE Caldera. The framework automates the calculation of Annualized Loss Expectancy (ALE) through Monte Carlo simulations, using real-time threat data and adversarial behavior emulation. MITRE Caldera simulates attacker Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs), which are fed into Splunk ES for correlation and event normalization. These outputs inform the FAIR model to generate probabilistic risk scores. This methodology enhances decision-making by providing measurable, data-driven insights into cybersecurity threats, enabling organizations to prioritize security investments based on quantified business impact.
steps_raw: This paper presents an integrated approach to automated cyber risk quantification by combining the Factor Analysis of Information Risk (FAIR) model with Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) and MITRE Caldera. The framework automates the calculation of Annualized Loss Expectancy (ALE) through Monte Carlo simulations, using real-time threat data and adversarial behavior emulation. MITRE Caldera simulates attacker Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs), which are fed into Splunk ES for correlation and event normalization. These outputs inform the FAIR model to generate probabilistic risk scores. This methodology enhances decision-making by providing measurable, data-driven insights into cybersecurity threats, enabling organizations to prioritize security investments based on quantified business impact.
